title: Foundation of the DTID Kill Chain
subtitle: Beginner | 20–30 min

slides.title: Overview
slides.content: The DTID Kill Chain is the foundation of all C-UAS operations. Before you can defeat a threat, you must Detect it, Track it, and Identify it. This module walks you through each phase using the Tutorial scenario — a single slow-moving commercial quadcopter on direct approach.

slides.title: Learning Objectives
content: Identify the four phases of the DTID kill chain
Use the L-Band surveillance radar to detect an inbound contact
Confirm a track and explain why correlation is required before engagement
Slew the EO/IR camera and classify the threat type
Select the appropriate countermeasure and engage
State the primary limitation of RF/PNT jamming

slides.title: Key Concepts
content: Sensor vs. effector — sensors detect and identify; effectors defeat
Why you identify before you engage: ROE and engagement authority
EO/IR camera: thermal vs. daylight mode
RF/PNT jamming is ineffective against autonomous (INS-navigated) threats

slides.title: Standards
content: Radar detection: contact detected within 10s of entering radar range
Track confirmation: confirmed before engagement — zero unconfirmed engagements
Camera ID: correct threat classification before defeat action
Effector selection: RF Jammer (not JACKAL) for commercial quad
Engagement: drone neutralized before reaching protected area
